title: CLI doctor --layer flag + bilingual contract report renderer
description: Add --layer=team|personal|all flag to fab doctor (mutex with --cite-coverage only — applies to coverage report rendering). Extend doctor's bilingual renderer (writeIssueSection or equivalent) to display new contract_metrics section with per-(layer,type) cross-tab. Honor layer filter at metric aggregation level (passed through to runDoctorCiteCoverage options).

files.target: citty command args definition + flag handler + report renderer (locate via grep --cite-coverage flag)
files.change: Add args entry: layer: { type: 'string', description: 'Filter cite metrics by KB layer: team|personal|all (default all)' }. Validate value ∈ {'team','personal','all'} else exit with error. Pass option to runDoctorCiteCoverage. Renderer: after existing rc.20 sections (recalled_unverified etc.), add contract section with header from i18n cite-coverage.contract.header. Render sub-table per (layer, type): for each layer (team/personal) show decisions/pitfalls counts, contract_with/missing/hard_violated, skip_count bucketed. Render skipped_bootstrap_drift message in red if status='skipped:bootstrap_drift'. Render cite_id_unresolved as separate '⚠' line.

Test cases: (1) --layer=team filter passes through to server; (2) --layer=invalid rejects with error; (3) renderer outputs contract section when contract_metrics present; (4) renderer outputs skipped message when status='skipped:bootstrap_drift'; (5) renderer outputs [team — review] vs [personal — fyi] suffixes; (6) renderer outputs skip_count histogram with i18n labels; (7) bilingual mode honors current locale config. ≥7 cases.

implementation: Read packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts to map current --cite-coverage flag + renderer entry point.
Add --layer arg with citty schema; validate value at command entry.
Pass layer option through to runDoctorCiteCoverage call.
Extend renderer: locate writeIssueSection or equivalent rendering helper; add contract section emitter that reads i18n keys per TASK-09.
Cross-tab renderer iterates per_layer_type, emits formatted rows (use existing table helper if present, else plain string concat).
Add ≥7 integration test cases per files[].change directive.
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test doctor-cite-coverage` to confirm green.

reference.examples: rc.20 TASK-07 introduced --cite-coverage flag + renderer; rc.24 mirrors structure for --layer flag + contract section.

rationale.chosen_approach: Single flag --layer parallel to existing --client. Renderer is additive — preserves rc.20 output layout.
alternatives_considered: Subcommand fab doctor cite-contract (rejected: doctor is flat-flag by design)
JSON-only output mode (rejected: bilingual stdout is core UX)
decision_factors: i18n keys from TASK-09 ready to consume
rationale.tradeoffs: Coverage report output gets longer; mitigated by clear section headers + skip-section-when-empty logic.

manual_checks: Manual: fab doctor --cite-coverage --layer=team → verify team-only output
Manual: fab doctor --cite-coverage with drift → verify skipped message visible
success_metrics: Renderer emits contract section per spec

criteria: doctor.ts contains arg 'layer' with enum values
doctor.ts passes layer option to runDoctorCiteCoverage
Renderer emits cite-coverage.contract.header section when contract_metrics present
Renderer suppresses contract section when contract_metrics_status='awaiting_marker' AND all counts 0
Renderer shows 'skipped (bootstrap drift — run `fab install`)' when status='skipped:bootstrap_drift'
Commit msg: 'feat(rc24): doctor --layer flag + contract report renderer (TASK-10)'
